_about: Cross-engine simulation parity tolerances (issue #15). THE single source of truth for how much numerical divergence between the native reference engine (ngspice, pinned in ENGINE_VERSIONS) and the in-browser WASM engine (eecircuit-engine / ngspice 45.2) is accepted before the sim-parity CI job fails. This file is NOT a golden fixture: it is parity CONFIG, not oracle output, so scripts/export_golden.py's --check skips it (it is in the exporter's IGNORED_PARITY_FILES set). Every entry that loosens beyond `defaults` MUST carry a written `justification`; 'it was flaky' is not a justification (AGENTS.md / issue #15 guardrail).

engine_pins._about: Recorded for the job summary and the version-drift gate (issue #15 amendment 1). The native engine version MUST equal tests/golden_corpus/ENGINE_VERSIONS; the browser engine version is informational (a bump is an oracle-first PR). compare_reports.py fails immediately if native_ngspice != the ENGINE_VERSIONS pin, BEFORE any numeric comparison, because different ngspice versions have different default tolerances and stepping behaviour and comparing across them manufactures noise that pressures tolerance-loosening.

defaults._about: Applied to every numeric field unless a field/design rule overrides. rtol/atol match the #14 report-parity tolerance (rtol 1e-3) with a small absolute floor; DC/op values are compared relatively. Transient waveforms are NEVER compared point-by-point — the report already reduces each probed signal to assertion-relevant measurements (final/min/max + their times), and THOSE are what is compared (issue #15: measurement-level comparison is the contract).

time_of_min.justification: DIVERGENCE A (formalized honestly). On a numerically FLAT signal every sample time is a valid argmin/argmax, so the reported extremum-TIME is engine-arbitrary while the extremum VALUE is exact. Concrete corpus case: esp32_battery_sensor/battery_adc_nominal probes `battery_sense`, which is flat at 1.04211V across the ENTIRE `.tran 1u 500ms` window (0..0.5s); native ngspice 42 reports time_of_max=0s (first sample) and eecircuit 45.2 reports 2.28e-06s (a different equally-valid sample) — the VALUE 1.04211V is byte-identical. The abs tolerance is therefore applied ONLY to the `time_of_min`/`time_of_max` fields and is sized to span the whole transient window (max 500ms in the corpus), with 2x headroom -> 1.0s. It does NOT relax the extremum VALUE (compared at rtol) nor the report STRUCTURE (byte-exact), so it cannot mask a real stats bug: a signal that is NOT flat has a well-defined extremum time that both engines must agree on within this window, and the value/structure checks stay strict.

time_of_max.justification: See time_of_min: same divergence A, same flat-signal engine-arbitrary argmax, same 1.0s window-spanning abs tolerance. Value and structure remain strict.

expected_divergences._about: NARROW, self-invalidating per-design exceptions for genuine converge-vs-NOT (or converge-on-different-rung) divergences (NOT value tolerances). Each entry names ONE design + ONE test and pins the EXACT convergence shapes both engines produce today. compare_reports.py requires BOTH engines to still produce those exact shapes: if the browser starts converging on rung 1 (matching native), or diverges differently, the exception no longer matches and the job FAILS, forcing this entry to be removed or re-justified. It is not a blanket skip — every OTHER field of the excepted report is still compared normally.

complex_bms.justification: DIVERGENCE B (formalized honestly, post-#94). Native ngspice 42 solves charge_cycle's operating point on rung 1 (as-written), but the browser's eecircuit 45.2 reports `singular matrix: check node charge_ctrl` on rung 1 and only converges after the #94 browser-side ladder injects the manual-documented .options (gmin/source-stepping, or Gear+relaxed reltol at rung 4). The browser now converges (backend=ngspice, convergence.converged=true) but the winning rung is >= 2, so `aids_required` is true and the convergence.note carries the standard 'numerical aids required' disclosure. The measurement stats agree with native within rtol on the probed nets; the divergence is confined to the convergence.attempts array (rung >=2 in browser vs 1 native) and the aids_required / rung / note fields. This is NOT absorbed by a numeric tolerance (rung is a schema integer, not a physical value); it is pinned as a documented, narrow exception.
complex_bms.follow_up: The exception collapses if the two engines' rung-1 behaviour ever aligns (both converge on rung 1). It stays STALE if the browser regresses to terminal non-convergence (browser_converged flips false again) — the comparator flags the shift and the entry must be removed or re-justified.

per_design._about: Reserved for design-specific numeric-tolerance overrides (rtol/atol) beyond `defaults`. EMPTY today: every converging corpus design agrees with the native reference within the default rtol (the corpus is DC/op-dominated, so the only real divergences are A and B above). Any future entry here MUST carry a `justification` explaining the ROOT CAUSE (usually .options or an engine-version behaviour), never 'flaky'.
